name: Temporal Equivalence Principle: A Blind-Prediction Residual Test in Multiply-Imaged Supernovae
description: SN Refsdal provides a rare blind-prediction test of potential-dependent temporal propagation: seven GR lens-modelling teams predicted the SX reappearance delay before the image was observed, while the later Kelly et al. (2023) measurement provides an independent comparator. This single-system probe reduces to a single-contrast measurement: 99.9% of the predicted proxy-model signal resides in the long-baseline S4--SX contrast, with effective dimensionality D_eff ≈ 2.0; the inner Einstein cross serves as a null region. The algebraic loop identity is insensitive to a uniform Mass Sheet Degeneracy, but the empirical blind-prediction residual remains lens-model-limited. The primary directional test is a Wilcoxon signed-rank test on seven non-zero blind residuals, all positive (p = 0.0078, ~2.4 sigma), matching the log-magnification proxy prediction. The supplementary all-eight-model Wilcoxon gives p = 0.0039 (2.8 sigma). Hierarchical Bayesian comparison remains inconclusive (BF ≈ 1.06 fixed-alpha, BF ≈ 0.49 free-alpha) at current lens-model uncertainties. The corrected Kelly+2023 S4 blind predictions infer a lensing-sector coupling alpha_inferred = -0.114 +/- 0.034 (3.39 sigma from GR), exceeding the all-sector proxy (~-0.055) by 1.75 sigma; the probative content lies in sign consistency across independent modelling groups.
